meta.title: Equivalent Air Depth Table
meta.source: US Navy Diving Manual Rev 7 Change A (2018)
meta.coverage_notes: Full standard USN air depth ladder (40-190 fsw in the tabulated increments) crossed with the full Navy-authorized nitrox O2 range (25%-40% O2, in 2% steps). Every cell is derived deterministically from the algebraic EAD formula EAD = (D + 33) * (FN2 / 0.79) - 33 (FN2 = 1 - FO2), then rounded UP to the nearest depth in this table's own depth ladder (this mirrors how the printed Table 10-1 rounds EAD to the next air-table depth). Cells where ppO2 = FO2 * (D/33 + 1) exceeds the 1.4 ata working ppO2 limit are omitted from the row (not present as a key): the Navy does not authorize normal nitrox use beyond 1.4 ata, so no EAD is tabulated there. Omitting (rather than storing null) preserves the existing engine.nitrox.lookup_ead() consumption contract, which falls back to the live algebraic formula for any depth/FO2 not present in a row -- that fallback path still carries the plan_nitrox_dive() ppO2 guard/warning for these over-ceiling combinations.
meta.unverified_warning: Nitrox EAD table derived from the US Navy EAD formula (2026-07-04); the derivation is exact, but cross-check the O2/depth grid against your Rev 7 manual before use.
